dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal emphasizes trend-following over counter-trend trading. Core rule: prefer entries aligned with prevailing trend (ADX >= 15 indicates strong trend). Counter-trend entries only permitted when ADX < 15 (weak/no trend environment). Entry logic enforces trend strength threshold before initiating any position. Exit when ADX falls below 15, signaling weakening trend. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT because rule applies to both long and short entries depending on trend direction. Position sizing for counter-trend trades normalized to 1.0x (no penalty). Source text reinforces theme of staying 'positioned in the right direction of a trend' and liquidating quickly if thesis changes.

source_rule_text: A trader should avoid initiating trades counter to the prevailing trend, as they statistically underperform trend-aligned trades, despite occasional outlier successes.
